road contractor died yesterday at

| failure.

tiffs in the suit to compel an &C
counting of her property.

gon land fraud cases has been up
held by the circuit court of appeals.

and assign the latter to separate

After predicting her death a few
days before it occurred, Salina Ha-
| gerty, Of Walla Walla, died at the
age of 81. She had lived at Walla
Walla for the past 80 years.

At Loomis. Wash, yesterday
morning Ellis Belanger shot and
killed his six year old brother Ce-
ell, Ellis was loading the gun when
it was discharged. He is 16 years
old.


Evidence of collusion between
Senator Mitchell and Binger Her
mann in securing the creation OF
forest reserves in Oregon was
given yesterday in Hermann's trial
| by George Sorenson, of Portland.

dent of Spokane, was yesterday APP
pointed to the state board of edu~
| cation. County superintendent

cago fire protection ordinances by
a Judge in that city, it seems alto
| gether unlikely that any punish
mcnt can be melted out to the par

gence for the terrible loss of life
at the Iroquois theater.
Tacoma estimates its losses in
wheat shipments this year on ac-
count of the railroad blockade at
more than 81 100000, and to water
front labor $50 1000 Exporters claim
there is yet no certain prospect Of
getting wheat to tidewater.

J. p. Morgan yesterday arranged
with Roosevelt for an interview be-
tween the president and four rail
road presidents, Mellen, McRea.
Newman and Hughitt They are
alarmed at the widespread agita-
ton for reduction in rates and want
to coach the president on how to
head 1t off.


John s. McMillin, deposed state
railroad commissioner, has return
ed to the state treasury $4,000 of
salary the legislative investigating
committee decided he did not earn


last year McMilin had given his
time to private business. mean
while drawing his state pay and
expenses.
